text:
Admission Date:  2013-07-05     Discharge Date:  1983-02-22

Date of Birth:   2008-10-04     Sex:  M

Service:

HISTORY OF THE PRESENT ILLNESS:  The patient is a 61-year-old
male with a history of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation,
borderline hypertension, asthma, who presented for an
elective carotid stent.  The patient was found to have atrial
fibrillation in 1997-10-05 when he presented to his PCP
with dyspnea on exertion and he was referred to Dr. Sally
Garcia for evaluation.  On physical examination, the
patient had a left carotid bruit and was referred for carotid
Doppler ultrasound.  He had an ultrasound in 2010-08-28
with the left internal carotid artery with 80-90% stenosis
and right internal carotid artery with 40% stenosis.  The MRI
of the head on 2001-02-05 was normal for signal within the
arterial arteries of the anterior and posterior circulation.
No evidence of vascular occlusion, stenosis, or aneurysm that
was greater than 3 mm in size.  The patient reports an
episode approximately five years ago of transient blurred
vision of both eyes lasting approximately 15 minutes.  No
history of dysarthria or dysphagia and




                          [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] Reed, M.D.  38657
